name: Blueberry Jam
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash and trim the rhubarb, then roughly chop it.
2. Cook the chopped rhubarb in a pan gently until it starts to soften.
3. Stir in the preserving sugar and dissolve it completely.
4. Add the blueberries and bring the mixture to a boil.
5. Boil the jam rapidly for up to 20 minutes until it reaches setting point.
6. Allow the jam to cool slightly, then pour into clean jars that have been warmed.
7. Cover, label, and store.
(Test for setting point: Place a spoonful of the cooled jam on a plate and press your finger onto the surface; if it wrinkles, the jam is ready.)
